segments.text: Then Bildad the Shuhite spoke up and said,
How much longer will you go on talking like this? The words coming out of your mouth are a lot of hot air!
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: Does God pervert justice? Does the Almighty pervert what is right?
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: Your children must have sinned against him, and so they deserved the punishment he inflicted on them.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: But if you pray to God and ask for his help,
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: if you live a clean life and do what is right, then he would act to make things right for you in your home.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: Though you began with next to nothing, you will end up with so much!
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: Why don't you ask what previous generations discovered, examine what our ancestors found out? We were born yesterday and don't know anything!
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: Our days on earth fade as quickly as a passing shadow.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: Won't they teach you and explain what they know?
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: Can papyrus grow where there is no marsh? Can reeds grow without water?
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: Even without being cut, while they are still flowering, they wither faster than grass.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: This is what happens to everyone who forgets God. The hopes of those who live without God come to nothing.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: Their confidence is like holding on to a flimsy spider's web.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: They look to their home to provide security, but it provides no support. They try to hang onto it, but it doesn't last.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: Those who live without God are like a luxuriant plant growing in the sun, spreading its shoots all over the garden.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: It twists its roots down through the stones, and holds on to rock.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: But when it's cut down, the place where it was disowns it, saying, ‘I never even saw you.’
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: Its life is over, and others spring up from the earth to take its place.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: Look, God doesn't reject someone who is innocent, and he doesn't support those who are guilty.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: He can make you laugh with happiness again and shout for joy.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: Those who hate you will be put to shame, and the place where the wicked live will be destroyed.
segments.speaker: Unknown
